# ui-leaf IPC Protocol Reference

## Overview

ui-leaf communicates with its caller over **line-delimited JSON** on stdin/stdout. Each message is a single JSON object followed by `\n`. Every message (both directions) carries `"version":"1"` as a top-level field. The binary rejects any message without a valid `version` field and emits an `error` outbound message.

**Stream shape:**

```
stdin  → line 1: InboundConfig (spawn config)
       → lines 2+: InboundMessage (runtime commands)
stdout ← OutboundMessage (events and responses)
GET /events ← SseMessage (server-sent events to the browser)
```

**Error semantics:**

- `OutboundError` without a `phase` tag is **fatal**: the binary exits 1 after emitting it.
- `OutboundError` with `phase:"build"` is **non-fatal**: the previous view is preserved and the mount stays alive.
- `OutboundError` with `phase:"runtime"` is **fatal**: the binary exits 1.

## Versioning Policy

The current protocol version is **`"1"`**. Version is a string const, not a number, to allow future alphanumeric identifiers.

**Additive changes (minor / non-breaking):** New optional fields on existing message types, new message types added to a `oneOf`, new SSE event types. Callers that ignore unknown fields continue to work without modification.

**Breaking changes (new major version):** Removing or renaming required fields, changing the meaning of an existing `type` discriminant, changing the version literal itself. A breaking change increments `version` (e.g. `"2"`) and is hosted at a new `$id` URI in the schema.

**SSE channel versioning:** SSE event payloads (`GET /events`) are unversioned — no `version` field appears on the SSE frame. This is a known asymmetry: the SSE channel is a server-push side channel for the browser, not part of the stdin/stdout IPC contract. Breaking SSE changes follow the same semver rules as the rest of the protocol.

---

## Inbound: Config (spawn config, stdin line 1)

The **first** line of stdin is always an `InboundConfig` object. It has no `type` discriminant — it is identified purely by position in the stream.

### InboundConfig

First line of stdin: config object that spawns a mount. No type discriminator; identified by position in the stream.

**Example**

```json
{
  "version": "1",
  "view": "Dashboard",
  "viewsRoot": "/home/user/myapp/views",
  "data": {
    "count": 0,
    "label": "start"
  },
  "mutations": [
    "increment",
    "reset"
  ],
  "title": "My App Dashboard",
  "port": 0,
  "openBrowser": true
}
```

**Fields**

| Field | Type | Req | Description | Constraints |
|---|---|:---:|---|---|
| `version` | `"1"` (const) | ✓ | Protocol version literal. A new $id hosts future breaking versions. | — |
| `view` | string | ✓ | View name (resolved relative to viewsRoot) or inline TSX source string. | — |
| `viewsRoot` | string | ✓ | Absolute path to the directory containing named view files. | — |
| `data` | any |  | Initial data object passed to the view component as props (any JSON value). | — |
| `mutations` | array |  | Names of mutation handlers the view is allowed to call. | — |
| `title` | string |  | Browser tab title. | — |
| `port` | integer |  | TCP port to bind. 0 = OS-assigned. | min: 0, max: 65535 |
| `openBrowser` | boolean |  | Whether to open a browser tab automatically on startup. | — |
| `shell` | `"tab"` \| `"app"` |  | macOS open-in shell mode. | — |
| `csp` | string |  | Content Security Policy preset name or raw policy string. | — |
| `heartbeatTimeoutMs` | integer |  | Milliseconds before a missing browser heartbeat closes the mount. Default: 5000. | min: 0 |
| `startupGraceMs` | integer |  | Milliseconds to wait for a first browser connection before emitting disconnected. | min: 0 |

---

## Inbound Messages (stdin lines 2+)

All subsequent stdin lines after the config are `InboundMessage` objects. Each carries `"version":"1"` and a `type` discriminant.

### `result`

Caller's response to a mutate request: success path. Correlated by id.

**Example**

```json
{
  "version": "1",
  "type": "result",
  "id": 1,
  "value": {
    "ok": true
  }
}
```

**Fields**

| Field | Type | Req | Description | Constraints |
|---|---|:---:|---|---|
| `version` | `"1"` (const) | ✓ | Protocol version literal. A new $id hosts future breaking versions. | — |
| `type` | `"result"` (const) | ✓ | Discriminant: successful mutation response. | — |
| `id` | integer | ✓ | Mutation request ID echoed back from the OutboundMutate message. | — |
| `value` | any |  | Return value from the mutation handler (any JSON value, including undefined/absent for void mutations). | — |


### `error`

Caller's response to a mutate request: error path. Correlated by id.

> Note: distinct from `OutboundError`. This is a mutation reply correlated by `id`, not a stream-level error.

**Example**

```json
{
  "version": "1",
  "type": "error",
  "id": 1,
  "message": "handler threw: file not found"
}
```

**Fields**

| Field | Type | Req | Description | Constraints |
|---|---|:---:|---|---|
| `version` | `"1"` (const) | ✓ | Protocol version literal. A new $id hosts future breaking versions. | — |
| `type` | `"error"` (const) | ✓ | Discriminant: failed mutation response. | — |
| `id` | integer | ✓ | Mutation request ID echoed back from the OutboundMutate message. | — |
| `message` | string | ✓ | Human-readable error message from the mutation handler. | — |


### `update`

Replace in-memory data and emit a data-updated SSE event to connected browser tabs.

**Example**

```json
{
  "version": "1",
  "type": "update",
  "data": {
    "count": 42
  }
}
```

**Fields**

| Field | Type | Req | Description | Constraints |
|---|---|:---:|---|---|
| `version` | `"1"` (const) | ✓ | Protocol version literal. A new $id hosts future breaking versions. | — |
| `type` | `"update"` (const) | ✓ | Discriminant: data update. | — |
| `data` | any | ✓ | New data object to pass to the view component as props (any JSON value, including null). | — |


### `view`

Swap the view source on-the-fly; triggers a recompile and a view-swapped SSE reload.

**Example**

```json
{
  "version": "1",
  "type": "view",
  "source": "export default function View({name}){return <h1>{name}</h1>}"
}
```

**Fields**

| Field | Type | Req | Description | Constraints |
|---|---|:---:|---|---|
| `version` | `"1"` (const) | ✓ | Protocol version literal. A new $id hosts future breaking versions. | — |
| `type` | `"view"` (const) | ✓ | Discriminant: view swap. | — |
| `source` | string | ✓ | Self-contained TSX source string. Relative imports are not supported; bare-package imports (react, react-dom) work via the internal alias plugin. | — |


### `patch`

Atomically replace both data and view source. If the compile fails, neither takes effect and the previous state is preserved.

**Example**

```json
{
  "version": "1",
  "type": "patch",
  "data": {
    "count": 42
  },
  "view": {
    "source": "export default function View({name}){return <h1>{name}</h1>}"
  }
}
```

**Fields**

| Field | Type | Req | Description | Constraints |
|---|---|:---:|---|---|
| `version` | `"1"` (const) | ✓ | Protocol version literal. A new $id hosts future breaking versions. | — |
| `type` | `"patch"` (const) | ✓ | Discriminant: atomic data + view patch. | — |
| `data` | any | ✓ | New data object (any JSON value, including null). | — |
| `view` | object | ✓ | New view descriptor. | — |


### `reopen`

Re-invoke open(url) to launch a fresh browser tab at the same mount URL.

**Example**

```json
{
  "version": "1",
  "type": "reopen"
}
```

**Fields**

| Field | Type | Req | Description | Constraints |
|---|---|:---:|---|---|
| `version` | `"1"` (const) | ✓ | Protocol version literal. A new $id hosts future breaking versions. | — |
| `type` | `"reopen"` (const) | ✓ | Discriminant: reopen browser tab. | — |


### `close`

Terminate the mount cleanly (caller-initiated close). The binary emits OutboundClosed with reason:"caller" and exits 0.

**Example**

```json
{
  "version": "1",
  "type": "close"
}
```

**Fields**

| Field | Type | Req | Description | Constraints |
|---|---|:---:|---|---|
| `version` | `"1"` (const) | ✓ | Protocol version literal. A new $id hosts future breaking versions. | — |
| `type` | `"close"` (const) | ✓ | Discriminant: close mount. | — |


### `ping`

Caller heartbeat. The binary silently acknowledges and keeps the mount alive. No reply is emitted.

**Example**

```json
{
  "version": "1",
  "type": "ping"
}
```

**Fields**

| Field | Type | Req | Description | Constraints |
|---|---|:---:|---|---|
| `version` | `"1"` (const) | ✓ | Protocol version literal. A new $id hosts future breaking versions. | — |
| `type` | `"ping"` (const) | ✓ | Discriminant: caller heartbeat ping. | — |


---

## Outbound Messages (stdout)

The binary writes `OutboundMessage` objects to stdout, one per line. Each carries `"version":"1"` and a `type` discriminant.

### `ready`

Emitted once when the HTTP server is listening and the view URL is known. The caller should open a browser at this URL if openBrowser was false.

**Example**

```json
{
  "version": "1",
  "type": "ready",
  "url": "http://127.0.0.1:5810",
  "port": 5810
}
```

**Fields**

| Field | Type | Req | Description | Constraints |
|---|---|:---:|---|---|
| `version` | `"1"` (const) | ✓ | Protocol version literal. A new $id hosts future breaking versions. | — |
| `type` | `"ready"` (const) | ✓ | Discriminant: server ready. | — |
| `url` | string | ✓ | Full URL the view is accessible at, including the auth token fragment. | — |
| `port` | integer | ✓ | TCP port the server bound to. | min: 1, max: 65535 |


### `mutate`

Emitted when the view invokes a named mutation. The caller must respond with InboundMutateResult or InboundMutateError carrying the same id.

**Example**

```json
{
  "version": "1",
  "type": "mutate",
  "id": 1,
  "name": "saveFile",
  "args": [
    "./report.csv"
  ]
}
```

**Fields**

| Field | Type | Req | Description | Constraints |
|---|---|:---:|---|---|
| `version` | `"1"` (const) | ✓ | Protocol version literal. A new $id hosts future breaking versions. | — |
| `type` | `"mutate"` (const) | ✓ | Discriminant: mutation request. | — |
| `id` | integer | ✓ | Unique per-mount mutation request ID for correlating the response. | — |
| `name` | string | ✓ | Mutation handler name as declared in InboundConfig.mutations. | — |
| `args` | any | ✓ | Arguments passed to the mutation handler by the view (any JSON value). | — |


### `disconnected`

Browser tab heartbeat stopped; mount stays alive, waiting for a reconnect.

**Example**

```json
{
  "version": "1",
  "type": "disconnected"
}
```

**Fields**

| Field | Type | Req | Description | Constraints |
|---|---|:---:|---|---|
| `version` | `"1"` (const) | ✓ | Protocol version literal. A new $id hosts future breaking versions. | — |
| `type` | `"disconnected"` (const) | ✓ | Discriminant: browser disconnected. | — |


### `reconnected`

Browser reconnected after a prior disconnected event.

**Example**

```json
{
  "version": "1",
  "type": "reconnected"
}
```

**Fields**

| Field | Type | Req | Description | Constraints |
|---|---|:---:|---|---|
| `version` | `"1"` (const) | ✓ | Protocol version literal. A new $id hosts future breaking versions. | — |
| `type` | `"reconnected"` (const) | ✓ | Discriminant: browser reconnected. | — |


### `closed`

Mount terminated. The binary exits after emitting this message. Exit code is 0 for caller/signal, 1 for error.

**Example**

```json
{
  "version": "1",
  "type": "closed",
  "reason": "caller"
}
```

**Fields**

| Field | Type | Req | Description | Constraints |
|---|---|:---:|---|---|
| `version` | `"1"` (const) | ✓ | Protocol version literal. A new $id hosts future breaking versions. | — |
| `type` | `"closed"` (const) | ✓ | Discriminant: mount closed. | — |
| `reason` | `"caller"` \| `"signal"` \| `"error"` | ✓ | Why the mount closed: caller sent close, OS signal received, or unrecoverable error. | — |


### `error`

Structured error output. Errors without a phase tag are fatal (exit 1); phase:"build" errors are non-fatal (previous view preserved).

**Example**

```json
{
  "version": "1",
  "type": "error",
  "message": "handler threw: file not found"
}
```

**Fields**

| Field | Type | Req | Description | Constraints |
|---|---|:---:|---|---|
| `version` | `"1"` (const) | ✓ | Protocol version literal. A new $id hosts future breaking versions. | — |
| `type` | `"error"` (const) | ✓ | Discriminant: error. | — |
| `phase` | `"build"` \| `"runtime"` |  | Error phase tag, if applicable. Omitted for general errors. | — |
| `message` | string | ✓ | Human-readable error message. | — |


### `view-swapped`

Emitted by the binary after a successful view swap (triggered by InboundView or InboundPatch). The caller uses this to resolve a pending setView() or patch() call.

**Example**

```json
{
  "version": "1",
  "type": "view-swapped"
}
```

**Fields**

| Field | Type | Req | Description | Constraints |
|---|---|:---:|---|---|
| `version` | `"1"` (const) | ✓ | Protocol version literal. A new $id hosts future breaking versions. | — |
| `type` | `"view-swapped"` (const) | ✓ | Discriminant: view swap completed. | — |


---

## SSE Events (`GET /events`)

The browser subscribes to `GET /events` (requires `X-UI-Leaf-Token` header). Each event is a standard SSE frame: `data: <JSON>\n\n`. SSE events are **not** versioned — no `version` field appears in the payload. See the versioning policy section for details.

### `data-updated`

SSE event broadcast to all connected browser tabs when in-memory data is replaced (via InboundUpdate or InboundPatch). The browser view should re-render with the new data.

**Example**

```json
{
  "type": "data-updated",
  "data": {
    "count": 42
  }
}
```

**Fields**

| Field | Type | Req | Description | Constraints |
|---|---|:---:|---|---|
| `type` | `"data-updated"` (const) | ✓ | Discriminant: data-updated SSE event. | — |
| `data` | any | ✓ | The new data object passed to the view as props (any JSON value, including null). | — |


### `view-swapped`

SSE event broadcast to all connected browser tabs after a successful view recompile (via InboundView or InboundPatch). The browser performs a full page reload to pick up the new view bundle.

**Example**

```json
{
  "type": "view-swapped"
}
```

**Fields**

| Field | Type | Req | Description | Constraints |
|---|---|:---:|---|---|
| `type` | `"view-swapped"` (const) | ✓ | Discriminant: view-swapped SSE event. | — |


### `closing`

SSE event broadcast to all connected browser tabs immediately before the HTTP server stops. The browser can use this to show a 'mount closed' overlay.

**Example**

```json
{
  "type": "closing",
  "reason": "caller"
}
```

**Fields**

| Field | Type | Req | Description | Constraints |
|---|---|:---:|---|---|
| `type` | `"closing"` (const) | ✓ | Discriminant: closing SSE event. | — |
| `reason` | `"caller"` \| `"signal"` \| `"error"` | ✓ | Why the mount is closing: caller sent close, OS signal received, or unrecoverable error. | — |
